examples.title: Algorithm: Query GPU Device Properties
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating how to query NVIDIA GPU device properties using CUDA runtime API
examples.code:
ALGORITHM QueryGPUProperties
INPUT: None
OUTPUT: Display of all GPU device properties

BEGIN
 # Step 1: Get the number of CUDA-capable devices
 CALL cudaGetDeviceCount(deviceCount)
 IF deviceCount == 0 THEN
 PRINT "No CUDA-capable devices found"
 RETURN
 END IF

 # Step 2: Iterate through each device
 FOR dev FROM 0 TO deviceCount - 1 DO
 # Step 3: Get device properties
 CALL cudaGetDeviceProperties(prop, dev)

 # Step 4: Extract and display compute capability
 majorVersion = prop.major
 minorVersion = prop.minor
 PRINT "Device ", dev, ": ", prop.name
 PRINT " Compute Capability: ", majorVersion, ".", minorVersion

 # Step 5: Display hardware limits
 PRINT " SM Count: ", prop.multiProcessorCount
 PRINT " Max Threads per Block: ", prop.maxThreadsPerBlock
 PRINT " Max Threads per SM: ", prop.maxThreadsPerMultiProcessor
 PRINT " Warp Size: ", prop.warpSize
 PRINT " Global Memory: ", prop.totalGlobalMem, " bytes"
 PRINT " Shared Memory per Block: ", prop.sharedMemPerBlock
 PRINT " Registers per Block: ", prop.regsPerBlock
 PRINT " Max Grid Size: [", prop.maxGridSize[0], ", ", prop.maxGridSize[1], ", ", prop.maxGridSize[2], "]"
 PRINT " Max Block Dimensions: [", prop.maxThreadsDim[0], ", ", prop.maxThreadsDim[1], ", ", prop.maxThreadsDim[2], "]"

 # Step 6: Display memory characteristics
 PRINT " Memory Clock Rate: ", prop.memoryClockRate, " kHz"
 PRINT " Memory Bus Width: ", prop.memoryBusWidth, " bits"
 PRINT " L2 Cache Size: ", prop.l2CacheSize, " bytes"

 # Step 7: Map compute capability to architecture name
 IF majorVersion == 1 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: Tesla"
 ELSE IF majorVersion == 2 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: Fermi"
 ELSE IF majorVersion == 3 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: Kepler"
 ELSE IF majorVersion == 5 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: Maxwell"
 ELSE IF majorVersion == 6 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: Pascal"
 ELSE IF majorVersion == 7 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: ", (minorVersion == 0 ? "Volta" : "Turing")
 ELSE IF majorVersion == 8 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: Ampere"
 ELSE IF majorVersion == 9 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: Hopper"
 ELSE IF majorVersion >= 10 THEN
 PRINT " Architecture: Blackwell or newer"
 END IF
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the standard CUDA runtime API workflow for querying GPU device properties. It first obtains the number of available CUDA devices, then iterates through each device to retrieve its properties using cudaGetDeviceProperties(). The algorithm extracts key information including compute capability (major.minor version), hardware limits (threads per block, SM count), memory specifications, and maps the compute capability to the corresponding GPU architecture generation.

examples.title: Python: Query GPU Properties using PyCUDA
examples.description: Python implementation to query and display NVIDIA GPU properties using PyCUDA library
examples.code:
"""
NVIDIA GPU Device Query using PyCUDA
Queries and displays compute capability and hardware properties
"""

import pycuda.driver as cuda
import pycuda.autoinit

def get_architecture_name(major, minor):
 """Map compute capability to architecture generation name"""
 arch_map = {
 (1, 0): "Tesla", (1, 1): "Tesla", (1, 2): "Tesla", (1, 3): "Tesla",
 (2, 0): "Fermi", (2, 1): "Fermi",
 (3, 0): "Kepler", (3, 2): "Kepler", (3, 5): "Kepler",
 (5, 0): "Maxwell", (5, 2): "Maxwell",
 (6, 0): "Pascal", (6, 1): "Pascal", (6, 2): "Pascal",
 (7, 0): "Volta", (7, 5): "Turing",
 (8, 0): "Ampere", (8, 6): "Ampere", (8, 9): "Ampere",
 (9, 0): "Hopper",
 (10, 0): "Blackwell"
 }
 return arch_map.get((major, minor), f"Unknown (CC {major}.{minor})")

def query_gpu_properties():
 """Query and display all GPU device properties"""
 # Initialize CUDA driver
 cuda.init()
 device_count = cuda.Device.count()

 print("=" * 60)
 print(f"Found {device_count} CUDA-capable device(s)")
 print("=" * 60)

 for dev_id in range(device_count):
 # Get device by ID
 device = cuda.Device(dev_id)
 props = device.get_attributes()

 # Extract key properties
 name = device.name()
 major = props[pycuda.device_attribute.compute_capability_major]
 minor = props[pycuda.device_attribute.compute_capability_minor]
 sm_count = props[pycuda.device_attribute.MULTIPROCESSOR_COUNT]
 max_threads_block = props[pycuda.device_attribute.MAX_THREADS_PER_BLOCK]
 max_threads_sm = props[pycuda.device_attribute.MAX_THREADS_PER_MULTIPROCESSOR]
 warp_size = props[pycuda.device_attribute.WARP_SIZE]
 global_mem = props[pycuda.device_attribute.GLOBAL_MEMORY_BYTES]
 shared_mem_block = props[pycuda.device_attribute.MAX_SHARED_MEMORY_PER_BLOCK]
 regs_block = props[pycuda.device_attribute.MAX_REGISTERS_PER_BLOCK]
 clock_rate = props[pycuda.device_attribute.CLOCK_RATE]
 memory_clock = props[pycuda.device_attribute.MEMORY_CLOCK_RATE]
 mem_bus_width = props[pycuda.device_attribute.GLOBAL_MEMORY_BUS_WIDTH_IN_BITS]
 l2_cache = props[pycuda.device_attribute.L2_CACHE_SIZE]

 # Get max grid and block dimensions
 max_grid = device.get_attribute(pycuda.device_attribute.MAX_GRID_DIM_X)
 max_block_x = device.get_attribute(pycuda.device_attribute.MAX_BLOCK_DIM_X)

 # Display properties
 print(f"\nDevice {dev_id}: {name}")
 print("-" * 40)
 print(f" Compute Capability: {major}.{minor}")
 print(f" Architecture: {get_architecture_name(major, minor)}")
 print(f" SM Count: {sm_count}")
 print(f" Max Threads per Block: {max_threads_block}")
 print(f" Max Threads per SM: {max_threads_sm}")
 print(f" Warp Size: {warp_size}")
 print(f" Global Memory: {global_mem / (1024**3):.2f} GB")
 print(f" Shared Memory per Block: {shared_mem_block / 1024:.0f} KB")
 print(f" Registers per Block: {regs_block}")
 print(f" GPU Clock Rate: {clock_rate / 1e6:.2f} GHz")
 print(f" Memory Clock Rate: {memory_clock / 1e6:.2f} GHz")
 print(f" Memory Bus Width: {mem_bus_width} bits")
 print(f" L2 Cache Size: {l2_cache / 1024:.0f} KB")
 print(f" Max Grid Size (X): {max_grid}")
 print(f" Max Block Dim (X): {max_block_x}")

 # Feature availability checks
 print(" Feature Support:")
 if major >= 3:
 print(" - Dynamic Parallelism: YES")
 else:
 print(" - Dynamic Parallelism: NO")
 if major >= 7:
 print(" - Tensor Cores: YES")
 else:
 print(" - Tensor Cores: NO")
 if major >= 7 and minor >= 5:
 print(" - RT Cores: YES")
 else:
 print(" - RT Cores: NO")
 if major >= 8:
 print(" - Async Copy: YES")
 else:
 print(" - Async Copy: NO")
 if major >= 9:
 print(" - Thread Block Clusters: YES")
 else:
 print(" - Thread Block Clusters: NO")

 print("\n" + "=" * 60)

if __name__ == "__main__":
 query_gpu_properties()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation uses PyCUDA to query GPU properties at runtime. It demonstrates how to access device attributes, convert compute capability to architecture names, and display comprehensive hardware information. The code also includes feature availability checks based on compute capability versions (e.g., Tensor Cores require CC 7.0+, RT Cores require CC 7.5+, Thread Block Clusters require CC 9.0+). This is essential for writing portable CUDA applications that adapt to different GPU generations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of devices

examples.title: C: Query GPU Device Properties with CUDA Runtime
examples.description: Complete C program demonstrating cudaGetDeviceProperties() to query compute capability and hardware limits
examples.explanation: This comprehensive C program demonstrates the CUDA runtime API for querying GPU device properties. It uses cudaGetDeviceProperties() to retrieve all hardware information including compute capability, SM count, thread limits, memory specifications, and clock rates. The program includes feature availability checks based on compute capability versions and maps version numbers to architecture names. The CUDA_CHECK macro provides proper error handling. This is the fundamental way to write portable CUDA code that adapts to different GPU generations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of devices queried

examples.title: C: Conditional Compilation and Targeting Specific Compute Capabilities
examples.description: Demonstrates compiler flags and __CUDA_ARCH__ macro for targeting specific GPU architectures
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates multiple methods for targeting specific NVIDIA compute capabilities in CUDA programs. Method 1 uses runtime queries via cudaGetDeviceProperties() for maximum flexibility. Method 2 uses the __CUDA_ARCH__ preprocessor macro for compile-time conditional compilation, where different code paths are compiled based on the target architecture. Method 3 shows architecture-specific kernel specialization using preprocessor directives to select different kernel implementations. Method 4 demonstrates cudaFuncGetAttributes() to query kernel characteristics at runtime. The program also includes a reference for common compilation flags like -arch, -gencode, and explains how to create fat binaries that support multiple GPU architectures in a single executable.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for runtime queries; kernel execution O(n)
